name: Pakistan

visa_free.notes: All Maldivian nationals (ordinary passport holders) may enter Pakistan without a visa for up to three months under visa abolition agreement. Entry 26 in the official dgip.gov.pk table (as of 21-02-2024): 'Maldives - Three Months - All Nationals of the Country'.

All Nepali nationals (ordinary passport holders) may enter Pakistan without a visa for up to one month under visa abolition agreement. Entry 32 in the official dgip.gov.pk table (as of 21-02-2024): 'Nepal - One Month - All Nationals of the Country'.

All Bahraini nationals (ordinary passport holders) may enter Pakistan without a visa for up to 90 days for business and tourism purposes, with multiple exit/re-entry permitted. Policy announced by Prime Minister of Pakistan in late July 2024, effective 23 August 2024. Confirmed by Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs press release and EY Tax Alert (29 August 2024) citing official Pakistani government announcement. No prior visa application or Electronic Travel Authorization required - air ticket sufficient.

All Kuwaiti nationals (ordinary passport holders) may enter Pakistan without a visa for up to 90 days for business and tourism purposes, with multiple exit/re-entry permitted. Policy announced by Prime Minister of Pakistan in late July 2024, effective 23 August 2024. Confirmed by Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs press release (Embassy of Pakistan in Kuwait) and EY Tax Alert (29 August 2024). No prior visa application required - air ticket sufficient.

All Omani nationals (ordinary passport holders) may enter Pakistan without a visa for up to 90 days for business and tourism purposes, with multiple exit/re-entry permitted. Policy announced by Prime Minister of Pakistan in late July 2024, effective 23 August 2024. Confirmed by Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs press release and EY Tax Alert (29 August 2024). No prior visa application required - air ticket sufficient.

All Qatari nationals (ordinary passport holders) may enter Pakistan without a visa for up to 90 days for business and tourism purposes, with multiple exit/re-entry permitted. Policy announced by Prime Minister of Pakistan in late July 2024, effective 23 August 2024. Confirmed by Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs press release and EY Tax Alert (29 August 2024). No prior visa application required - air ticket sufficient.

All Saudi Arabian nationals (ordinary passport holders) may enter Pakistan without a visa for up to 90 days for business and tourism purposes, with multiple exit/re-entry permitted. Policy announced by Prime Minister of Pakistan in late July 2024, effective 23 August 2024. Confirmed by Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs press release and EY Tax Alert (29 August 2024). No prior visa application required - air ticket sufficient.

All UAE nationals (ordinary passport holders) may enter Pakistan without a visa for up to 90 days for business and tourism purposes, with multiple exit/re-entry permitted. Policy announced by Prime Minister of Pakistan in late July 2024, effective 23 August 2024. Confirmed by Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs press release and EY Tax Alert (29 August 2024). No prior visa application required - air ticket sufficient.

e_visa.notes: Pakistan Online Visa System (POVS) at visa.nadra.gov.pk is open to citizens of 175 countries (as enumerated on dgip.gov.pk). List-B countries (India, Somalia) are excluded. Armenia, Israel and Taiwan are not part of any list. Standard processing time is approximately 7-10 working days at Pakistan missions abroad (or up to 20 business days per Fragomen March 2026 alert). The Visa Prior to Arrival (VPA) free fast-track program and the Visa on Arrival (VOA) program were both suspended effective 1 January 2026 - only the standard paid e-visa route remains operative. Russia is listed with an asterisk on the dgip.gov.pk e-visa table (possible restriction or note). Nationals of Afghanistan, China, Japan, Iceland, North Korea, Slovakia, Malaysia, UAE, and Saudi Arabia reportedly pay no visa fee (except work visas) but must still apply via POVS portal.

visa_policy.notes: Pakistan has bilateral visa abolition/exemption agreements with 49 countries (per Ministry of Interior and dgip.gov.pk as of 21-02-2024). Of these, only 2 countries (Maldives and Nepal) have long-standing bilateral agreements covering ALL passport types including ordinary. The 6 GCC countries (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE) were granted visa-free entry for ordinary passport holders effective 23 August 2024 by Prime Minister's announcement - confirmed by Pakistan MoFA press release and EY Tax Alert. All other bilateral agreements in the dgip.gov.pk 49-country table cover diplomatic and/or official passport holders only. The Visa Prior to Arrival (VPA) free program and the Visa on Arrival (VOA) program were both suspended effective 1 January 2026 per multiple consistent sources (confirmed still suspended as of June 2026, no reinstatement announced) - e-visa (POVS) is now the only operative route for most nationalities. GCC nationals are exempt from this suspension and retain their visa-free entry.

conditional_access.conditions: Foreign nationals of Pakistani origin holding a valid Pakistan Origin Card (POC) issued by NADRA under Section 11 of NADRA Ordinance, 2000 do not require a visa to enter Pakistan. Stay permitted for duration of card validity. Eligible categories: former Pakistani citizens, foreign nationals whose parents/grandparents are/were Pakistani, foreigners married to Pakistani citizens (excluding nationals of India, Bangladesh, unrecognised states or enemy countries), and dependent children of foreign spouses of Pakistani citizens. Not applicable to Indian or Bangladeshi nationals.

Holders of Austrian diplomatic and official passports may enter Pakistan without a visa for up to three months. Entry 2 in dgip.gov.pk visa abolition table (as of 21-02-2024).
Holders of Azerbaijani diplomatic and official passports may enter Pakistan without a visa for up to one month. Entry 3 in dgip.gov.pk visa abolition table (as of 21-02-2024).

Note: Ordinary passport holders of Bahrain are now separately covered as visa-free under the MoFA GCC press release (effective 23 August 2024) - see visa_policy.visa_free.

Holders of Kyrgyz diplomatic and official passports may enter Pakistan without a visa for up to two months. Entry 23 in dgip.gov.pk visa abolition table (as of 21-02-2024).

cbi.notes: Pakistan does not operate a formal Citizenship-by-Investment (CBI) program. There is a provision under Section 20 of the Pakistan Citizenship Act 1951 allowing Commonwealth citizens to apply for Pakistani citizenship after remitting Rs. 5 million in foreign exchange (verified by State Bank of Pakistan), but approval is discretionary and not guaranteed - this is not a structured CBI program. Pakistan has dual nationality agreements with 22 specific countries (UK, US, Canada, Australia, France, Italy, Belgium, Iceland, New Zealand, Finland, Egypt, Jordan, Syria, Switzerland, Netherlands, Sweden, Ireland, Bahrain, Denmark, Germany, Norway, Luxembourg) under Pakistan Citizenship (Amendment) Bill 2024.

programs.name: Long Term Residency (LTR) Scheme
programs.notes: Established under the Foreigners (Long Term Residency) Order, 2025. Grants foreign investors and their families residency in Pakistan for 5, 7, or 10 years, renewable. Minimum investment of USD 50,000 must be materialized within one year of approval through authorized banking channels. Each additional dependent (up to 7 - spouse, children under 25, parents) requires an additional USD 50,000. Annual renewal requires USD 50,000. LTR provides residency rights only - does not grant nationality or citizenship. Priority sectors: IT/technology, renewable energy, export-led manufacturing, agriculture, industrial development including SEZs. LTR Card issued by NADRA as proof of residency. LTR Digital Portal to go live; apply via invest.gov.pk. Confirmed from invest.gov.pk search result summary - direct page scrape timed out.

programs.name: SIFC Investor Visa
programs.notes: Special Investment Facilitation Council (SIFC) investor visa. Short-term: up to 3 years; long-term: up to 5 years (extendable by 2 additional years). No publicly disclosed investment threshold. SIFC recommendation letter required for visas over 1 year. For non-SIFC investor visas under the Business-Investor category: investment in SECP-registered companies of not less than USD 50,000 or real estate investment exceeding USD 100,000. Processing: 1-14 business days. Long-term categories allow legal residence. After 5 years legal residence and mastering an official language, foreigners can apply for citizenship (per visa.nadra.gov.pk).

programs.name: SIFC Business Visa
programs.eligibility: Citizens of all countries (BVL and non-BVL); intent to conduct business or invest in Pakistan, particularly in priority sectors such as mining and gold industries. SIFC recommendation letter may be required.
programs.notes: Issued under the Special Investment Facilitation Council (SIFC) framework. Short-term: single-entry up to 6 months; long-term: multiple-entry up to 5 years (extendable by 2 years). Operated alongside SIFC Investor Visa to attract foreign investment in priority sectors.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa
visa_types.purpose: For leisure, sightseeing, and recreational travel to Pakistan. Issued to foreign nationals who intend to visit Pakistan for tourism purposes.
visa_types.notes: Applied for via Pakistan Online Visa System (POVS) at visa.nadra.gov.pk. Available to nationals of approximately 175 countries (List A). The Visa on Arrival (VOA) and Visa Prior to Arrival (VPA) programs were suspended effective 1 January 2026 - only standard online e-visa route is available. Nationals of Afghanistan, China, Japan, Iceland, North Korea, Slovakia, Malaysia, UAE, and Saudi Arabia reportedly pay no visa fee. Processing time ~7 business days at missions abroad or up to 20 business days per Fragomen (March 2026).

visa_types.name: Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals traveling to Pakistan for commercial, trade, or business meetings. Also covers the Business Visa List (BVL) countries with facilitated access.
visa_types.notes: Applied for via POVS portal. Fee is approximately 150% of the tourist/visit visa fee for most nationalities (per Consulate General LA). Business Visa List (BVL) countries may have facilitated processing. Requires documentation of business purpose, invitation letters, or company correspondence.

visa_types.name: SIFC Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued under the Special Investment Facilitation Council (SIFC) framework for foreign investors and business persons targeting Pakistan's priority investment sectors including mining, gold, and IT.
visa_types.notes: Short-term: single-entry up to 6 months; long-term: multiple-entry up to 5 years (extendable by 2 additional years). SIFC recommendation letter may be required. Available to citizens of all countries, including both BVL and non-BVL countries. Priority sectors: mining, gold, IT/technology, energy.

visa_types.name: SIFC Investor Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign investors making significant investments in Pakistan under the Special Investment Facilitation Council framework, particularly in priority sectors such as technology, energy, and manufacturing.
visa_types.notes: Short-term: up to 3 years; long-term: up to 5 years (extendable by 2 additional years). SIFC recommendation letter required for visas over 1 year. For non-SIFC investor visas: investment in SECP-registered companies of not less than USD 50,000 or real estate investment exceeding USD 100,000. After 5 years legal residence and mastering an official language, foreigners can apply for citizenship per visa.nadra.gov.pk.

visa_types.name: Work/Employment Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals seeking to take up employment with a Pakistani organization or company. Requires sponsorship from a Pakistani employer.
visa_types.notes: Requires approval from employer and relevant government ministry. Fee approximately 150% of tourist visa rate (per Consulate General LA fee structure). Separate fee structure applies for US nationals. All visa applications must be submitted through the POVS online portal; manual processing discontinued.

visa_types.name: Student Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals admitted to an educational institution in Pakistan to pursue academic studies.
visa_types.notes: Requires prior approval from the Ministry of Education and Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan. Must have confirmed admission to a recognized Pakistani institution. Applied through POVS portal. Details page at Consulate General LA was marked 'under construction' as of June 2026.

visa_types.name: Family Visit Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals visiting family members residing in Pakistan. Covers spouse, children, parents, and other close relatives.
visa_types.notes: Requires invitation letter from the family member in Pakistan detailing purpose, duration, and cities of visit. Bank statements (3 months) and proof of family relationship required. Applied via POVS portal. For Indian nationals, fee is USD 192 per Consulate General LA. Afghan nationals have visa fee waived.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals transiting through Pakistan en route to a third country without leaving the airport or for a short stopover.
visa_types.notes: Transit without a visa permitted for up to 24 hours in some cases (per Pakistan Embassy Russia). Standard transit visa allows short stopover of up to 72 hours. Applied via POVS portal. Requires confirmed onward ticket and valid visa/residence permit for destination country.

visa_types.name: Medical Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals traveling to Pakistan to receive medical treatment at a recognized Pakistani healthcare facility.
visa_types.notes: Requires letter from a recognized Pakistani medical institution confirming admission for treatment. Applied via POVS portal. May be extended in-country if treatment continues longer than anticipated.

visa_types.name: NGO Representatives Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals representing non-governmental organizations working in Pakistan on approved development, humanitarian, or charitable projects.
visa_types.notes: Requires prior approval of Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan. NGO must be registered and approved to operate in Pakistan. Applied via POVS portal. Separate NOC (No Objection Certificate) may be required from relevant federal ministries.

visa_types.name: Journalist / Film / TV / Documentary Team Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign journalists, filmmakers, television crews, and documentary teams seeking to work in Pakistan on media projects.
visa_types.notes: Requires prior approval of Ministry of Information, Government of Pakistan. Press accreditation and project documentation required. Applied via POVS portal. Separate NOC from PEMRA or Ministry of Information may be required for filming activities.

visa_types.name: Missionary Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals traveling to Pakistan for religious missionary activities under an officially recognized and approved religious organization.
visa_types.notes: Requires prior approval of Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan. Religious organization must be officially recognized in Pakistan. Applied via POVS portal. Strict scrutiny applies; approval is discretionary.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic / Official Visa
visa_types.purpose: For holders of diplomatic or official passports traveling to Pakistan on government business, including members of diplomatic missions, consular posts, and international organizations.
visa_types.notes: 49 countries have bilateral visa abolition agreements with Pakistan covering diplomatic and/or official passport holders (per dgip.gov.pk as of 21-02-2024). Visa-free duration ranges from 30 to 90 days depending on the country. Members of diplomatic/consular missions and their families are exempt for the full duration of assignment. Processed through the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, not the standard POVS portal.

validation_notes: CORRECTION 1 (critical): Removed Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and UAE from visa_policy.visa_free. The official dgip.gov.pk 49-country visa abolition table (as of 21-02-2024, scraped June 2026) confirms that only Maldives and Nepal have agreements covering ALL nationals (ordinary passport holders). GCC countries appear only in the diplomatic/official section. The prior record erroneously listed these 6 GCC nations as visa-free for ordinary passport holders - moved to conditional_access as diplomatic/official passport entries (already present there) or flagged as unverified.
CORRECTION 2: Updated Maldives max_stay_days from null to 90 (three months). The official dgip.gov.pk table entry 26 explicitly states 'Three Months - All Nationals of the Country'.

CORRECTION 3: Cleared visa_policy.visa_on_arrival entirely. Both the VOA and VPA (Visa Prior to Arrival) programs were suspended effective 1 January 2026 per multiple consistent sources (Fragomen March 2026, VisasNews, Young Pioneer Tours, PassportIndex). The suspension has not been reversed as of June 2026. The dgip.gov.pk policy page still lists the VOA framework as policy text but this does not reflect the operational reality post-January 2026.

CORRECTION 4: Updated e_visa country count from '192 countries' to '175 countries'. The authoritative dgip.gov.pk page explicitly lists 175 countries eligible for e-visa, with List-B (India, Somalia) excluded and Armenia, Israel, Taiwan not part of any list. The prior record's '192 countries' figure was unsourced and incorrect.

CORRECTION 5: Removed the erroneous duplicate aggregated conditional_access entry that listed all diplomatic/official countries in a single object (lines 178-184 of prior record). Each country now has its own properly-sourced individual entry.
CORRECTION 6: Austria - confirmed as 'Diplomatic & Official Passport Holders' per dgip.gov.pk table. Prior record was correct.

CORRECTION 7: Oman - does NOT appear in the official 49-country dgip.gov.pk table (as of 21-02-2024). Retained in conditional_access but flagged as unverified from primary source. Oman appears in the VOA-50 list and BVL list but these are separate from visa abolition for ordinary passports.

CORRECTION 8: Saudi Arabia - does NOT appear in the official 49-country dgip.gov.pk table (as of 21-02-2024). Retained in conditional_access flagged as unverified. Saudi Arabia appears in BVL and VOA-50 lists.

VERIFICATION CONFIRMED: Nepal 30 days (one month) - correct. Maldives three months (90 days) - correct. All 49 diplomatic/official country entries verified against authoritative dgip.gov.pk table scraped June 2026.

CORRECTION 2026-06-27: Added 6 GCC countries (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE) to visa_policy.visa_free for ordinary passport holders. Prior record (2026-06) incorrectly classified these as diplomatic/official only. Pakistan MoFA official press release confirmed visa-free entry for all GCC nationals (ordinary passports) for tourism and business, up to 90 days, multiple entry, effective 23 August 2024. The dgip.gov.pk bilateral table (dated 21-02-2024) did not reflect this later policy change which came after that table's last update. Source: mofa.gov.pk/press-releases/gcc-citizens-now-enjoy-visa-free-travel-to-pakistan-for-business-and-tourism (confirmed via EY Tax Alert 29 Aug 2024 citing Pakistan PM announcement).

VERIFICATION CONFIRMED 2026-06-27: VOA/VPA suspension since 1 January 2026 remains in force - no reinstatement as of June 2026. Confirmed via multiple travel media sources and search of official gov.pk domains. GCC nationals explicitly exempt from this suspension and retain their visa-free entry.

VERIFICATION CONFIRMED 2026-06-27: LTR scheme USD 50,000 minimum confirmed via invest.gov.pk FAQs (loaded via search result). invest.gov.pk direct page still timing out but details confirmed via Google-indexed official page summaries.

CORRECTION 2026-06-29: Added Italy to conditional_access for diplomatic passport holders. Pakistan and Italy signed a mutual visa abolition agreement for diplomatic passport holders on 2 June 2026 at the Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs in Rome. Confirmed by MoFA press release, Dawn, Arab News PK, Geo.tv, Business Recorder, The News, The Diplomatic Insight. Agreement covers diplomatic passports ONLY - official and ordinary passports are not covered. Entry-into-force date and max stay duration not yet published in official text. Italy was previously listed in the data_quality.gaps as 'reportedly signed June 2026 but not yet confirmed'; it is now confirmed.

VERIFICATION CONFIRMED 2026-06-29: GCC 6-country visa-free (ordinary passports, 90 days, business/tourism) confirmed still in effect per visa.nadra.gov.pk search results and MoFA press release. Indexed official pages confirm ongoing validity as of June 2026.

VERIFICATION CONFIRMED 2026-06-29: VOA/VPA programs confirmed still suspended (no reinstatement as of June 2026) per YoungPioneerTours, VisasNews, Fragomen, AirTraveler.club, PassportIndex — all confirm suspension since 1 January 2026 with no reinstatement announced. Gulf nationals explicitly continue to receive visa-free access (not affected by VOA suspension).

VERIFICATION CONFIRMED 2026-06-29: Maldives and Nepal ordinary-passport visa-free confirmed unchanged from dgip.gov.pk visa abolition list (indexed by Google, 403 on direct fetch — page structure stable). E-visa for 175 countries confirmed stable per search results and NADRA portal indexing.
gaps: Oman and Saudi Arabia do not appear in the dgip.gov.pk 49-country bilateral visa abolition table (dated 21-02-2024), but their ordinary-passport visa-free status is now confirmed from the MoFA GCC press release (effective 23 Aug 2024) - the dgip.gov.pk table predates this policy change.

UAE diplomatic/official passport visa exemption under the older bilateral framework - not in the dgip.gov.pk 21-02-2024 table; now superseded by the MoFA GCC press release covering ordinary passports as well.

Italy diplomatic passport agreement (signed 2 June 2026): entry-into-force date and maximum stay duration not confirmed in any publicly accessible official text. Retained in conditional_access with max_stay_days: null until official gazette or MoFA operational guidance is published.
Full 175-country e-visa list per dgip.gov.pk; Russia has an asterisk (*) notation whose meaning is not specified on the scraped page - possible fee exemption or restriction.
VOA/VPA suspension (effective 1 January 2026) confirmed through travel media and industry sources but no official Pakistani government gazette notification was directly accessible.

LTR Digital Portal: invest.gov.pk direct page consistently times out; details confirmed only from search result summaries of the official page. Exact sector-specific minimums and application steps not confirmed from page body.
Turkey 'substitute visa' rule cited only by Wikipedia - no official Pakistani government page confirmed; not recorded.
UN Laissez-Passer and Interpol Travel Document exemptions - not confirmed from directly accessible official Pakistani government page; not recorded.
Pakistan's Commonwealth citizenship provision (Section 20, Pakistan Citizenship Act 1951) is a discretionary route, not a structured CBI program - included in cbi.notes only.

dgip.gov.pk and visa.nadra.gov.pk both returned 403 Forbidden or timeout on all direct fetch attempts during 2026-06-29 re-verification run; data confirmed via Google-indexed page summaries and authoritative secondary sources (MoFA press releases, Fragomen, Dawn, etc.).

outbound_official.note: No official Pakistan government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Extensive search (June 2026) of mofa.gov.pk, dgip.gov.pk, passport.gov.pk, interior.gov.pk, visa.nadra.gov.pk found no page analogous to India's mea.gov.in/vffin listing where Pakistani ordinary-passport holders can travel without a prior consular visa. The mofa.gov.pk/tabs/higher-education-commission page is indexed by Google as 'Travel Information' but was inaccessible (timeout) on all fetch attempts. The dgip.gov.pk site covers only inbound visa categories for foreigners entering Pakistan. Pakistan does not appear to publish an official origin-side outbound visa-exemption list for its own citizens.
